work.title: Work
work.description: Four projects. Professional, academic, and research-led. Each with its own texture, but the same underlying instinct: prototype to understand, not to present.
about.title: About
about.subtitle: Architect by training.
about.education: M.Des New Media Design · NID Gandhinagar
about.bio1: I came to design through architecture. Six years at SPA Vijayawada, licensed by the Council of Architecture, taught me to hold a system in my head at full scale while still caring about the door handle. Buildings are systems. So are interfaces. The patience is the same; the medium is faster.
about.bio2: I taught myself web development between architecture school and NID because I wanted a medium where I could test an idea the same afternoon I had it. My prototypes are not wireframes. They are functional, interactive, data-driven artifacts. When engineering builds from my prototypes directly, that's the intended workflow.
about.bio3: NID Gandhinagar gave me research rigour. The M.Des in New Media Design taught me to know when an instinct is actually an insight, and when it isn't. My graduation project, Realm of Elementals, was that discipline in full: nine months of research on care, decentering, and ecological identity, built into a WebAR experience.
about.bio4: Currently: Senior Product Designer at Trmeric, sole designer on the founding team. I own the full design surface of an AI-native enterprise SaaS platform, from information architecture to production-grade prototypes. I sit in product strategy as an equal, not as a service provider.
trmeric.title: Trmeric
trmeric.description: 23 surfaces. One AI-native enterprise platform. Designed and built as sole designer on the founding team.

home.heroTagline: I build the thing to understand the thing.
trmeric.phase.a.label: Shape
trmeric.phase.a.question: What should we build?
trmeric.phase.a.desc: Capture intent. Structure the demand. Tango turns an idea into a scoped initiative in 90 seconds instead of 3 months.
trmeric.phase.b.label: Plan
trmeric.phase.b.question: Who builds it, by when?
trmeric.phase.b.desc: Assign resources, set timelines, track budget. Force-graph visibility across the whole portfolio from the start.
trmeric.phase.c.label: Execute
trmeric.phase.c.question: Are we on track?
trmeric.phase.c.desc: Keep initiatives on track. Surface risks before they become blockers. Knowledge stays close to the work.
trmeric.phase.d.label: Evaluate
trmeric.phase.d.question: Did it deliver value?
trmeric.phase.d.desc: Outcome tracking, recognition, and portfolio closure. The loop closes back to the next demand.
trmeric.feature.demand.title: Demand Owner Flow
trmeric.feature.demand.oneLiner: From a blank demand idea to a scoped, resourced, Tango-validated initiative.
trmeric.feature.portfolio.title: Portfolio Monitor
trmeric.feature.portfolio.oneLiner: Multi-portfolio force graph with persona-contextual views for CIOs, Portfolio Leaders, and Resource Managers.
trmeric.feature.projectmgr.title: Project Manager + RAID
trmeric.feature.projectmgr.oneLiner: Project status and execution management built on one principle: Up-level is the only path forward.
trmeric.feature.trucible.title: Trucible · Knowledge OS
trmeric.feature.trucible.oneLiner: Wiki and Explorer modes for an enterprise context management system. Not a document store.
trmeric.feature.signals.title: Signals · D3 Force Graph
trmeric.feature.signals.oneLiner: An Obsidian-style force-directed signal map for early-warning detection across a project portfolio.
realm.subtitle: Where vanishing wings are kept alive.
realm.tagline: An augmented-reality experience & installation · Aravind J
realm.quote: The small things you do, for yourself and for the world around you, become a living creature you can watch grow. You live it. The realm shows it.
realm.pillar.1.title: Act
realm.pillar.1.desc: One real act a day, for yourself or the world.
realm.pillar.2.title: See
realm.pillar.2.desc: Your care grows into a living creature.
realm.pillar.3.title: Learn
realm.pillar.4.title: Become
realm.pillar.4.desc: Help nature, and it helps you back.
realm.problem.title: 01 · The Problem
realm.problem.body: We are told the planet is dying. And we feel nothing.
realm.problem.desc: The crisis is everywhere and felt nowhere. We hear the planet is collapsing, yet nothing we do each day seems to touch it. The damage is far away, slow, abstract. Our own choices feel too small to matter. So we look away. Not from cruelty, but because we can never see the line between what we do and what becomes of the world, or of ourselves. What you cannot see, you cannot care for.
realm.stat.1: % decline in butterfly numbers in 20 years
realm.stat.2: % of insect species in decline worldwide
realm.stat.3: pollinator species at risk of extinction

about.site.label: On this site
about.site.title: The form is the argument.
about.site.body: The architectural section metaphor, moving inward along a Z-axis, is the SPA Vijayawada self: structure, depth, intentional space. The particle ecosystem, organic and living with distinct behaviours per domain, is the NID self: research, ecology, care. The WebGL binding built in real time is the developer self that learned to build ideas the same day he had them.

trmeric.flagship.growTheMark.ariaLabel: Animated derivation of the trmeric mark: a rhizome cross-section photograph traces into an outline, averages into a ring form, then five golden-ratio rings stroke-draw themselves at their exact weights.
trmeric.flagship.growTheMark.caption: Rendered live, not a screenshot · photograph → traced outline → averaged form → five golden-ratio rings
trmeric.flagship.growTheMark.heading: The mark, growing itself.
trmeric.flagship.growTheMark.body: Everything on this page described as a step, here in one sequence: the photograph gives way to a traced outline, the outline averages into a ring form, and the five golden-ratio rings stroke-draw themselves at 12 / 9.71 / 7.85 / 6.35 / 4.99px, outer to inner.
page.trmericSlug.miniDemo.signals.kicker: Try it — the actual interaction, in miniature
page.trmericSlug.miniDemo.signals.body: This is the real hover-to-highlight model, at a smaller scale: three clusters standing in for a portfolio's worth of signals. Hover any node.
page.trmericSlug.miniDemo.projectManager: The sparkle icon's animation rate is the severity, not just its colour. Watch the three states below run at their real cadence.
page.trmericSlug.miniDemo.trucible.body: Hover a tier. Each one exists because a percentage score can't tell a Demand Manager whether to trust what Tango just told them.
page.trmericSlug.miniDemo.trucible.tiers: Multiple connected, recent sources. Tango answers from here first and shows its citations.
A handful of sources, some aging. Tango answers but flags the gaps it's inferring across.
One or two sources, thin coverage. Tango answers cautiously and asks for a second source.
No connected sources yet. Tango says so directly instead of guessing.
page.trmericSlug.prototype.kicker: Live prototype — real code, not a mockup
content.trucible.intro:
Trucible started with a direction from Sid: "this is a context management system, not a document management system." That sentence rewrote the product. We weren't building Notion. We were building the memory layer of the organisation.

The dual-mode design (Wiki and Explorer) emerged from that distinction. Wiki mode presents Trucible-generated articles, synthesised from connected sources, maintained by Tango, readable by humans. Explorer mode is the database view: folder trees, graph visualisations, list tables. The same knowledge, two interfaces for two mental models.

The hardest problem wasn't the interface, it was trust. An AI-synthesised article is only useful if the reader believes it, and belief needs evidence, not just fluent prose.
content.trucible.heroStat.after: Cited context Tango can act on
content.trucible.heroStat.label: What a connected source becomes
content.trucible.personaTraits.0.label: Goal
content.trucible.personaTraits.0.detail: Get enough organisational context to act credibly in the next meeting, without reading six months of Slack and email history first.
content.trucible.personaTraits.1.label: Biggest fear
content.trucible.personaTraits.1.detail: Re-opening a decision that was already made and reversed, because nobody could find the thread that explained why.
content.trucible.personaTraits.2.label: What success looks like
content.trucible.personaTraits.2.detail: An answer with a source attached, in under a minute, that they can defend if a colleague pushes back on it.
content.trucible.personaTraits.3.label: AI posture
content.trucible.personaTraits.3.detail: Will trust Tango's synthesis exactly as far as its citations hold up under a click-through, and no further.
content.trucible.principles.0.title: Context, not documents
content.trucible.principles.0.detail: Trucible doesn't store files. It maintains a living, Tango-synthesised understanding of what the organisation knows, with the source documents underneath as evidence.
content.trucible.principles.1.title: Two mental models, one dataset
content.trucible.principles.1.detail: Wiki mode is for reading a synthesised answer; Explorer mode is for browsing the raw structure. Same knowledge graph, never forked between them.
content.trucible.principles.2.title: Provenance is not optional
content.trucible.principles.2.detail: Every Wiki article traces back to its connected sources. An unsupported claim is treated as a bug in the synthesis, not an acceptable shortcut.
content.trucible.principles.3.title: Freshness decays visibly
content.trucible.principles.3.detail: A context tier isn't set once and forgotten. Sources age and connections go stale, and the tier badge reflects that in real time, not just at creation.
content.trucible.innovations.wiki.title: Wiki mode as a written destination, not a search result
content.trucible.quotes.0: I joined mid-project and Trucible got me to "not embarrassing myself on the client call" in twenty minutes, not two weeks.
content.trucible.quotes.1: Deep Root or Seedling tells me in one glance whether I can quote this to a client or whether I need to go verify it first.
content.signals.introExtra: Signals isn't a standalone destination. It lives embedded beside the Project Manager, and a signal click surfaces the related project card inline, so investigating a risk never costs a navigation.
content.signals.heroStat.label: Time to spot a systemic risk cluster
content.signals.principles.0.title: Hover over click
content.signals.principles.1.title: Physics carries meaning
content.signals.principles.2.title: Never lose your place
content.signals.principles.3.title: Zero-navigation action
content.signals.quotes.0: I used to build this map in my head from six different status reports. Now I open one screen and the map already exists.
content.signals.quotes.1: The red cluster in the centre told me in ten seconds what would have taken a week of one-on-ones to piece together.
